openmeter/registry/builder.purpose: Single-function package providing GetEntitlementRegistry, a non-Wire factory that wires all entitlement-domain adapters, connectors, and services into *registry.Entitlement. Used by balance-worker and test suites that need entitlement infrastructure without importing app/common.

patterns.name: Options struct as constructor input
patterns.description: All external dependencies are grouped into EntitlementOptions (DatabaseClient, StreamingConnector, Logger, MeterService, CustomerService, Publisher, Tracer, Locker, EntitlementsConfiguration). New parameters go on this struct, not as direct function args.

patterns.name: Bottom-up adapter initialisation order
patterns.description: DB adapters (featureDBAdapter, entitlementDBAdapter, grantDBAdapter, balanceSnapshotDBAdapter) are created first, then connectors depending on them, then the top-level entitlement service. Never create a connector before its adapter dependencies.

patterns.name: Register hooks immediately after connector construction
patterns.description: RegisterHooks is called immediately after each connector is constructed. Both meteredEntitlementConnector and entitlementConnector get their own RegisterHooks call.

patterns.name: Return fully populated registry struct by pointer
patterns.description: GetEntitlementRegistry returns *registry.Entitlement with every field explicitly populated. Any new connector/adapter added to registry.Entitlement must be assigned here or callers get nil fields and runtime panics.
patterns.example: return &registry.Entitlement{Feature: featureConnector, GrantRepo: grantDBAdapter, MeteredEntitlement: meteredEntitlementConnector, Entitlement: entitlementConnector, ...}

patterns.name: transactionManager via enttx.NewCreator
patterns.description: The Ent transaction manager is created with enttx.NewCreator(opts.DatabaseClient) and passed into CreditConnectorConfig.TransactionManager. Never pass the raw *db.Client as a transaction manager.

key_file_guides.role: The only file. Defines EntitlementOptions and GetEntitlementRegistry; owns the full wiring sequence for entitlement-domain infrastructure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new adapter/connector requires (1) constructing it in correct bottom-up order, (2) passing it to dependent downstream constructors, (3) assigning it to the returned registry struct. Skipping step 3 leaves nil fields and runtime panics.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from this package — it must remain independent to avoid import cycles with test suites.
Skipping hook registration after connector construction — entitlementsubscriptionhook and credithook are mandatory for correct burn-down and subscription sync.
Passing opts.DatabaseClient directly as a TxCreator — always wrap with enttx.NewCreator.
Adding business logic or conditional wiring inside GetEntitlementRegistry — it is a pure factory; conditionals belong in the caller.
Returning a partially populated *registry.Entitlement — every field must be set or downstream nil-dereferences occur at runtime.

decisions.decision: Single-function package instead of a Wire provider set.
decisions.rationale: balance-worker and test suites need entitlement infrastructure without importing app/common Wire sets; a plain constructor avoids import cycles while giving a fully wired registry.

decisions.decision: EntitlementOptions flat struct over variadic functional options.
decisions.rationale: All dependencies are mandatory; a flat struct makes missing fields panic early on zero-value pointers and avoids option-function indirection.

code_examples.scenario: Construct the entitlement registry for a worker or test
code_examples.code:
import (
  registrybuilder "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/registry/builder"
  "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/registry"
)

reg := registrybuilder.GetEntitlementRegistry(registrybuilder.EntitlementOptions{
  DatabaseClient:            db,
  EntitlementsConfiguration: cfg,
  StreamingConnector:        streamingConn,
  Logger:                    logger,
  MeterService:              meterSvc,
  CustomerService:           customerSvc,
  Publisher:                 publisher,
  Tracer:                    tracer,
  Locker:                    locker,
})
